text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Brief

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Edge Runtime vs. Fluid Compute: When to Use Which (And What Each Is Actually For)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: angle

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Edge Runtime is not deprecated—it's been repositioned as a specialized tool for latency-sensitive middleware, while Fluid Compute is now the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: default for application logic. Most developers are making architectural decisions based on outdated assumptions about runtime boundaries,

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: leading to either over-engineered Edge functions or missed latency optimizations.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: hook

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Your Edge function just hit a 1MB bundle limit in production, and you're wondering if you should rewrite everything in Node.js—or if that

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: defeats the point. The real question isn't which runtime is "better," but why Vercel now runs both on the same infrastructure and what that

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: changes about how you choose.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: audience

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: A senior frontend engineer or architect who has been using Edge Runtime for API routes, has seen the "migrate to Node.js" recommendation in the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: docs, and is uncertain whether to migrate existing workloads or how to decide for new projects.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: narrativeArc

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: B1 Establish the confusion: Vercel now recommends Node.js for "improved performance and reliability" but Edge Runtime still exists and is

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: actively maintained—developers don't know what to trust.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: B2 Reveal the shift: Both runtimes now run on Fluid Compute with the same Active CPU pricing model; the old performance and cost trade-offs

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: no longer apply, but new constraints (bundle size, API availability, execution model) do.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: B3 Introduce the decision framework: Edge Runtime is for middleware that must run before routing (auth, geolocation, A/B testing); Fluid

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Compute (Node.js) is for everything else—API routes, SSR, background jobs.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: B4 Provide concrete migration guidance: When to port Edge functions to Node.js, when to keep them, and how to structure a hybrid architecture

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: without duplication.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: technicalDetails

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 01 Edge Runtime constraints: 1MB (Hobby) / 2MB (Pro) / 4MB (Enterprise) bundle size after gzip; 25s response initiation with 300s streaming; no Node.js

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: built-ins except async_hooks, events, buffer, assert, util; no dynamic code execution (eval, new Function, WebAssembly.instantiate with buffers)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 02 Fluid Compute capabilities: optimized concurrency (multiple invocations per instance), bytecode caching (Node.js 20+), shared global state across

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: concurrent requests, 300s default duration (800s max on Pro/Enterprise), waitUntil for background work

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 03 Configuration: export const runtime = 'edge' vs export const runtime = 'nodejs' (default); preferredRegion for Edge, region in vercel.json for

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Node.js

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 04 Error handling differences: Edge isolates per-request; Fluid Compute shares process state and handles uncaught exceptions without crashing concurrent

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: requests

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: 05 Pricing: both use Active CPU model—CPU time while processing, not wall time; Edge previously had different pricing structure

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: whyNow

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Fluid Compute became the default for new projects on April 23, 2025, and the docs now explicitly recommend Node.js over Edge Runtime for general

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: use—developers are actively re-evaluating existing architectures and need authoritative guidance on what to migrate, what to keep, and why the

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: recommendation changed.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: (And When to Finally Migrate to Node.js)"

g.text: titleOptions claim

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: "Edge Runtime Isn't Dead—It's Just Not

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: for Your API Routes Anymore"

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: titleOptions question

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: "How to Choose Between Edge and Node.js

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Now That Both Run on Fluid Compute"

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Editorial note

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: This post fills a critical gap. The docs now recommend Node.js but don't explain the architectural reasoning, and community threads show confusion about

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: whether Edge is being sunset. The angle is defensible because it acknowledges Edge's continued relevance while clarifying its narrowed scope—this prevents

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: both unnecessary migration work and continued misuse.
